673b8d447c14188a841fa630.question: A character created by this author echoes Hamlet by saying, “now I want to be all the time and that's why all the time I must a little bit not-be.” Musician Paul Wilson translated a work by this author in which a character who is fired for helping her boss is told by him to find work in a theater. In that play by this author, George watches characters through (*) cracks in the walls and temporarily replaces the protagonist in his job. In a play by this author a character dons a papier-mache nose in order to mimic the bureaucratic Maxy Falk. A play by this author ends with Chorukor replacing the language Ptydepe because of how complicated it is. For 10 points, name this author who satirized the politics of his home country in The Memorandum and The Garden Party.
673b8d447c14188a841fa630.question_sanitized: A character created by this author echoes Hamlet by saying, "now I want to be all the time and that's why all the time I must a little bit not-be." Musician Paul Wilson translated a work by this author in which a character who is fired for helping her boss is told by him to find work in a theater. In that play by this author, George watches characters through (*) cracks in the walls and temporarily replaces the protagonist in his job. In a play by this author a character dons a papier-mache nose in order to mimic the bureaucratic Maxy Falk. A play by this author ends with Chorukor replacing the language Ptydepe because of how complicated it is. For 10 points, name this author who satirized the politics of his home country in The Memorandum and The Garden Party.

673b8d447c14188a841fa634.question: In a poem sometimes titled for one of these objects, a character has his name changed after helping the protagonist dig up three of these objects. Edward Pettit’s The Waning Sword compares the title object to ice, a candle, and one of these objects. Another poem claims that, “Hope was renewed / with cheer and bliss for those who were burning” after an action is performed with one of these objects. That poem is about an object that begins bleeding on the right side and is (*) bedecked with gems and gold. Along with Cynewulf’s Elene, that poem about a tree that becomes one of these objects appears in the Vercelli Codex. For 10 points, The Dream of the Rood is about what type of object, central to Christian imagery?
673b8d447c14188a841fa634.question_sanitized: In a poem sometimes titled for one of these objects, a character has his name changed after helping the protagonist dig up three of these objects. Edward Pettit's The Waning Sword compares the title object to ice, a candle, and one of these objects. Another poem claims that, "Hope was renewed / with cheer and bliss for those who were burning" after an action is performed with one of these objects. That poem is about an object that begins bleeding on the right side and is (*) bedecked with gems and gold. Along with Cynewulf's Elene, that poem about a tree that becomes one of these objects appears in the Vercelli Codex. For 10 points, The Dream of the Rood is about what type of object, central to Christian imagery?

673b8d447c14188a841fa645.leadin: After an earthquake causes a pipe to burst early in this novel, one character sees a woman who resembles his late wife holding on to the head of a statue leading him to start an affair with her. For 10 points each:
673b8d447c14188a841fa645.leadin_sanitized: After an earthquake causes a pipe to burst early in this novel, one character sees a woman who resembles his late wife holding on to the head of a statue leading him to start an affair with her. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel, narrated by Cecilia Brady, who is left heartbroken and fatherless, after her love interest fails to call off a hit on her father due to his death in a plane crash.

Although The Last Tycoon was left unfinished at this author’s death, his friend Edmund Wilson edited it for publication using his notes. Other characters created by this author include Dick and Nicole Diver, whose relationship mirrors this author’s tumultuous marriage.

This expert on Fitzgerald called Wilson’s version “adequate for salvaging Fitzgerald’s literary remains” in The Last of the Novelists. He also authored a biography of Fitzgerald titled Some Sort of Epic Grandeur.
parts_sanitized: Name this novel, narrated by Cecilia Brady, who is left heartbroken and fatherless, after her love interest fails to call off a hit on her father due to his death in a plane crash.

Although The Last Tycoon was left unfinished at this author's death, his friend Edmund Wilson edited it for publication using his notes. Other characters created by this author include Dick and Nicole Diver, whose relationship mirrors this author's tumultuous marriage.

This expert on Fitzgerald called Wilson's version "adequate for salvaging Fitzgerald's literary remains" in The Last of the Novelists. He also authored a biography of Fitzgerald titled Some Sort of Epic Grandeur.
answers_sanitized: The Love of the Last Tycoon

This Nobel Prize winner has been accused of plagiarism for his novel about an Italian scholar who is kidnapped to serve under Hoja.

Pamuk was also accused of plagiarizing this author’s historical fiction novel Ancient Evenings for My Name is Red. His other historical fiction includes The Executioner’s Song and The Naked and the Dead.
